title: HUG: Human Universal Grasping
summary: HUG is a flow-matching model that generates diverse human grasps for objects in RGB-D images, trained on 1M-HUGs — an egocentric dataset of 1 million frames spanning 6,707 object instances. The model predicts grasps parameterized by wrist translation, rotation, and hand pose, which can be retargeted to various robot hands. HUG outperforms state-of-the-art grasping baselines by +23% and +34%, achieving 62% success on unseen objects in real home environments.
